volumeTitle: Antigone. The Women of Trachis. Philoctetes. Oedipus at Colonus
label: Antigone (Section 933 - Section 1090)

sections.label: Section 933
sections.original: οἴμοι , θανάτου τοῦτʼ ἐγγυτάτω
sections.translation: Ah , no ! That command verges close on death .

sections.label: Section 935
sections.original: θαρσεῖν οὐδὲν παραμυθοῦμαι
sections.translation: I cannot console you with any hope that your doom is not to be fulfilled in that way .

sections.label: Section 937
sections.original: ὦ γῆς Θήβης ἄστυ πατρῷον
sections.translation: O city of my fathers , land of Thebes , and you gods , our ancestors ! I am led away now ; there is no more delay !

sections.label: Section 940
sections.original: λεύσσετε , Θήβης οἱ κοιρανίδαι
sections.translation: Look at me , you who are Thebes ’ lords — look at the only remaining daughter of the house of your kings . See what I suffer , and at whose hands , because I revered reverence ! Antigone is led away by the guards .

sections.label: Section 1.944
sections.original: ἔτλα καὶ Δανάας οὐράνιον φῶς
sections.translation: So too endured Danae in her beauty to change

sections.label: Section 1.945
sections.original: ἀλλάξαι δέμας ἐν χαλκοδέτοις αὐλαῖς ·
sections.translation: the light of the sky for brass-bound walls , and in that chamber , both burial and bridal , she was held in strict confinement . And yet was she of esteemed lineage , my daughter ,

sections.label: Section 1.950
sections.original: καὶ Ζηνὸς ταμιεύεσκε γονὰς χρυσορύτους .
sections.translation: and guarded a deposit of the seed of Zeus that had fallen in a golden rain . But dreadful is the mysterious power of fate — there is no deliverance from it by wealth or by war , by towered city , or dark , sea-beaten ships .

sections.label: Section 1.955
sections.original: ζεύχθη δʼ ὀξύχολος παῖς ὁ Δρύαντος ,
sections.translation: And Dryas ’ s son , the Edonian king swift to rage , was tamed in recompense for his frenzied insults , when , by the will of Dionysus , he was shut in a rocky prison . There the fierce and swelling force of his madness trickled away .

sections.label: Section 1.960
sections.original: ἀνθηρόν τε μένος . κεῖνος ἐπέγνω μανίαις
sections.translation: That man came to know the god whom in his frenzy he had provoked with mockeries . For he had sought to quell the god-inspired women and the Bacchanalian fire ,

sections.label: Section 1.965
sections.original: φιλαύλους τʼ ἠρέθιζε Μούσας .
sections.translation: and he angered the Muses who love the flute .

sections.label: Section 2.966
sections.original: παρὰ δὲ Κυανεᾶν πελάγει διδύμας ἁλὸς
sections.translation: And by the waters of the Dark Rocks , the waters of the twofold sea , are the shores of Bosporus and the Thracian city Salmydessus ,

sections.label: Section 2.970
sections.original: Σαλμυδησσός , ἵνʼ ἀγχίπτολις Ἄρης
sections.translation: where Ares , neighbor of that city , saw the accursed , blinding wound inflicted on the two sons of Phineus by his savage wife . It was a wound that brought darkness to the hollows , making them crave vengeance

sections.label: Section 2.975
sections.original: ἀραχθέντων , ὑφʼ αἱματηραῖς
sections.translation: for the eyes she crushed with her bloody hands and with her shuttle for a dagger .

sections.label: Section 2.977
sections.original: κατὰ δὲ τακόμενοι μέλεοι μελέαν πάθαν
sections.translation: Wasting away in their misery , they bewailed their miserable suffering

sections.label: Section 2.980
sections.original: κλαῖον , ματρὸς ἔχοντες ἀνύμφευτον γονάν ·
sections.translation: and their birth from their mother stripped of her marriage . But she traced her descent from the ancient line of the Erechtheids , and in far-distant caves she was raised amidst her father ’ s gusts . She was the child of Boreas ,

sections.label: Section 2.985
sections.original: Βορεὰς ἅμιππος ὀρθόποδος ὑπὲρ πάγου
sections.translation: running swift as horses over the steep hills , a daughter of gods . Yet she , too , was assailed by the long-lived Fates , my child .

sections.label: Section 988
sections.original: Θήβης ἄνακτες , ἥκομεν κοινὴν ὁδὸν
sections.translation: Princes of Thebes , we have come on a shared journey , two scouting the way by the eyes of one .

sections.label: Section 990
sections.original: αὕτη κέλευθος ἐκ προηγητοῦ πέλει .
sections.translation: For this is the method of travel for the blind , using a guide .

sections.label: Section 991
sections.original: τί δʼ ἔστιν , ὦ γεραιὲ Τειρεσία , νέον ;
sections.translation: What is it , old Teiresias ? What is your news ?

sections.label: Section 992
sections.original: ἐγὼ διδάξω , καὶ σὺ τῷ μάντει πιθοῦ .
sections.translation: I will tell you . You , obey the seer .

sections.label: Section 993
sections.original: οὔκουν πάρος γε σῆς ἀπεστάτουν φρενός .
sections.translation: It was not my habit before , at any rate , to stand apart from your will .

sections.label: Section 994
sections.original: τοιγὰρ διʼ ὀρθῆς τήνδʼ ἐναυκλήρεις πόλιν .
sections.translation: Therefore you captained this city on an upright course .

sections.label: Section 995
sections.original: ἔχω πεπονθὼς μαρτυρεῖν ὀνήσιμα .
sections.translation: I have felt and can attest your benefits .

sections.label: Section 996
sections.original: φρόνει βεβὼς αὖ νῦν ἐπὶ ξυροῦ τύχης .
sections.translation: Realize that once more now you are poised on fortune ’ s razor-edge .

sections.label: Section 997
sections.original: τί δʼ ἔστιν ; ὡς ἐγὼ τὸ σὸν φρίσσω στόμα .
sections.translation: What do you mean ? I shudder to hear you !

sections.label: Section 998
sections.original: γνώσει , τέχνης σημεῖα τῆς ἐμῆς κλύων .
sections.translation: You will understand , when you hear the signs revealed by my art . As I took my place on my old seat of augury

sections.label: Section 1000
sections.original: ἵζων , ἵνʼ ἦν μοι παντὸς οἰωνοῦ λιμήν ,
sections.translation: where all birds regularly gather for me , I heard an unintelligible voice among them : they were screaming in dire frenzy that made their language foreign to me . I realized that they were ripping each other with their talons , murderously — the rush of their wings did not lack meaning .

sections.label: Section 1005
sections.original: εὐθὺς δὲ δείσας ἐμπύρων ἐγευόμην
sections.translation: Quickly , in fear , I tried burnt-sacrifice on a duly-kindled altar , but from my offerings Hephaestus did not blaze . Instead juice that had sweated from the thigh-flesh trickled out onto the embers and smoked and sputtered ;

sections.label: Section 1010
sections.original: χολαὶ διεσπείροντο , καὶ καταρρυεῖς
sections.translation: the gall was scattered high up in the air ; and the streaming thighs lay bared of the fat that had been wrapped around them . Such was the failure of the rites that yielded no sign , as I learned from this boy . For he is my guide , as I am guide to others .

sections.label: Section 1015
sections.original: καὶ ταῦτα τῆς σῆς ἐκ φρενὸς νοσεῖ πόλις .
sections.translation: And it is your will that is the source of the sickness now afflicting the city . For the altars of our city and our hearths have one and all been tainted by the birds and dogs with the carrion taken from the sadly fallen son of Oedipus . And so the gods no more accept prayer and sacrifice at our hands ,

sections.label: Section 1020
sections.original: θεοὶ παρʼ ἡμῶν οὐδὲ μηρίων φλόγα ,
sections.translation: or the burning of thigh-meat , nor does any bird sound out clear signs in its shrill cries , for they have tasted the fatness of a slain man ’ s blood . Think , therefore , on these things , my son . All men are liable to err .

sections.label: Section 1025
sections.original: ἐπεὶ δʼ ἁμάρτῃ , κεῖνος οὐκέτʼ ἔστʼ ἀνὴρ
sections.translation: But when an error is made , that man is no longer unwise or unblessed who heals the evil into which he has fallen and does not remain stubborn . Self-will , we know , invites the charge of foolishness . Concede the claim of the dead . Do not kick at the fallen .

sections.label: Section 1030
sections.original: κέντει · τίς ἀλκὴ τὸν θανόντʼ ἐπικτανεῖν ;
sections.translation: What prowess is it to kill the dead all over again ? I have considered for your good , and what I advise is good . The sweetest thing is to learn from a good advisor when his advice is to your profit .

sections.label: Section 1033
sections.original: ὦ πρέσβυ , πάντες ὥστε τοξόται σκοποῦ
sections.translation: Old man , you all shoot your arrows at me , like archers at their mark , and I am not safe

sections.label: Section 1035
sections.original: ἄπρακτος ὑμῖν εἰμι · τῶν δʼ ὑπαὶ γένους
sections.translation: even from the plottings of the seer ’ s divine art , but by their tribe I have long been bought and sold and made their merchandise . Turn your profits , make your deals for the white gold of Sardis and the gold of India , if it pleases you , but you shall not cover that man with a grave ,

sections.label: Section 1040
sections.original: οὐδʼ εἰ θέλουσʼ οἱ Ζηνὸς αἰετοὶ βορὰν
sections.translation: not even if the eagles of Zeus wish to snatch and carry him to be devoured at the god ’ s throne . No , not even then , for fear of that defilement will I permit his burial , since I know with certainty that no mortal has the power to defile the gods .

sections.label: Section 1045
sections.original: πίπτουσι δʼ , ὦ γεραιὲ Τειρεσία , βροτῶν
sections.translation: But even the exceedingly clever , old Teiresias , falls with a shameful fall , when they couch shameful thoughts in fine phrasing for profit ’ s sake .

sections.label: Section 1048
sections.original: φεῦ .
sections.translation: Alas ! Does any man know , does any consider —

sections.label: Section 1049
sections.original: τί χρῆμα ; ποῖον τοῦτο πάγκοινον λέγεις ;
sections.translation: What is this ? What universal truth are you announcing ?

sections.label: Section 1050
sections.original: ὅσῳ κράτιστον κτημάτων εὐβουλία ;
sections.translation: — by how much the most precious of our possessions is the power to reason wisely ?

sections.label: Section 1051
sections.original: ὅσῳπερ , οἶμαι , μὴ φρονεῖν πλείστη βλάβη .
sections.translation: By as much , I think , as senselessness is the greatest affliction .

sections.label: Section 1052
sections.original: ταύτης σὺ μέντοι τῆς νόσου πλήρης ἔφυς .
sections.translation: Yet you came into being full of that disease .

sections.label: Section 1053
sections.original: οὐ βούλομαι τὸν μάντιν ἀντειπεῖν κακῶς .
sections.translation: I have no desire to trade insults with the seer .

sections.label: Section 1054
sections.original: καὶ μὴν λέγεις , ψευδῆ με θεσπίζειν λέγων .
sections.translation: Yet that is what you do in saying that I prophesy falsely .

sections.label: Section 1055
sections.original: τὸ μαντικὸν γὰρ πᾶν φιλάργυρον γένος .
sections.translation: Yes , for the prophet-clan was ever fond of money .

sections.label: Section 1056
sections.original: τὸ δʼ ἐκ τυράννων αἰσχροκέρδειαν φιλεῖ .
sections.translation: And the race sprung from tyrants loves shameful gain .

sections.label: Section 1057
sections.original: ἆρʼ οἶσθα ταγοὺς ὄντας ἃν λέγῃς λέγων ;
sections.translation: Do you know that you ramble so about your king ?

sections.label: Section 1058
sections.original: οἶδʼ · ἐξ ἐμοῦ γὰρ τήνδʼ ἔχεις σώσας πόλιν .
sections.translation: I am aware , since through me you have saved this city .

sections.label: Section 1059
sections.original: σοφὸς σὺ μάντις , ἀλλὰ τἀδικεῖν φιλῶν .
sections.translation: You are a wise seer , but fond of doing injustice .

sections.label: Section 1060
sections.original: ὄρσεις με τἀκίνητα διὰ φρενῶν φράσαι .
sections.translation: You will stir me to utter the dire secret in my soul .

sections.label: Section 1061
sections.original: κίνει , μόνον δὲ μὴ ʼπὶ κέρδεσιν λέγων .
sections.translation: Out with it ! But only if it is not for gain that you speak it .

sections.label: Section 1062
sections.original: οὕτω γὰρ ἤδη καὶ δοκῶ τὸ σὸν μέρος .
sections.translation: Indeed , I think I speak without mention of gain — where you are concerned .

sections.label: Section 1063
sections.original: ὡς μὴ ʼμπολήσων ἴσθι τὴν ἐμὴν φρένα .
sections.translation: Be certain that you will not trade in my will .

sections.label: Section 1064
sections.original: ἀλλʼ εὖ γέ τοι κάτισθι μὴ πολλοὺς ἔτι
sections.translation: Then know , yes , know it well ! You will not live through many more

sections.label: Section 1065
sections.original: τρόχους ἁμιλλητῆρας ἡλίου τελεῖν ,
sections.translation: courses of the sun ’ s swift chariot , before you will give in return one sprung from your own loins , a corpse in requital for corpses . For you have thrust below one of those of the upper air and irreverently lodged a living soul in the grave ,

sections.label: Section 1070
sections.original: ἔχεις δὲ τῶν κάτωθεν ἐνθάδʼ αὖ θεῶν
sections.translation: while you detain in this world that which belongs to the infernal gods , a corpse unburied , unmourned , unholy . In the dead you have no part , nor do the gods above , but in this you do them violence . For these crimes the avenging destroyers ,

sections.label: Section 1075
sections.original: λοχῶσιν Ἅιδου καὶ θεῶν Ἐρινύες ,
sections.translation: the Furies of Hades and of the gods , lie in ambush for you , waiting to seize you in these same sufferings . And look closely if I tell you this with a silvered palm . A time not long to be delayed will reveal in your house wailing over men and over women .

sections.label: Section 1080
sections.original: ἐχθραὶ δὲ πᾶσαι συνταράσσονται πόλεις ,
sections.translation: All the cities are stirred up in hostility , whose mangled corpses the dogs , or the wild beasts or some winged bird buried , carrying an unholy stench to the city that held each man ’ s hearth . There , now , are arrows for your heart , since you provoke me ,

sections.label: Section 1085
sections.original: ἀφῆκα θυμῷ καρδίας τοξεύματα
sections.translation: launched at you , archer-like , in my anger . They fly true — you cannot run from their burning sting . Boy , lead me home , so that he may launch his rage against younger men , and learn to keep a quieter tongue

sections.label: Section 1090
sections.original: τὸν νοῦν τʼ ἀμείνω τῶν φρενῶν ἢ νῦν φέρει .
sections.translation: and a better mind within his breast than he now bears . Exit Teiresias .
